Afternoon Birding Stroll
June 2 @ 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Afternoon Birding Stroll

Tuesdays, June 2-30, 4-5 pm Join a Naturalist for a late afternoon stroll on the trails in search of the various bird species that call the Garden home. Binoculars are available to borrow. 9:30 am - 11:00 am Recurring
Meadow Makers
June 3 @ 9:30 am - 11:00 am Recurring
Meadow Makers

Wednesdays, 9:30–11 am, May 20 – September 2, 2026 Join in the meadow-making in Theodore Wirth Regional Park! Stop by the meadow off the entrance drive to the Eloise Butler Wildflower Garden and join in the weeding and planting fun. No registration required. Stay for as little or as long as you’d like!

Trivia Night
June 3 @ 12:00 pm - 1:00 pm Recurring
Trivia Night

4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Invertebrate Discovery Walks
June 3 @ 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Invertebrate Discovery Walks

Wednesdays, June 3 – June 24, 4:00pm – 5:00pm Join a naturalist on a walk to discover the hidden kingdoms of insects and other invertebrates in the Garden! We'll learn about invertebrate identification, their behavior, and ecology as we observe them on the trails. No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

1 event, 4

5:00 pm - 6:00 pm Recurring
Flower Hour
June 4 @ 5:00 pm - 6:00 pm Recurring
Flower Hour

Thursdays, May – August, 5-6 pm Join a Naturalist for an hour-long evening walk through the Garden to see the seasonal wildflowers. The plants are the highlight here in this tour. Come learn and unwind in nature in the beautiful Garden. No registration necessary, meet at the Visitor Shelter.

1:00 pm - 5:00 pm
Juneteenth 2026
June 13 @ 1:00 pm - 5:00 pm
Juneteenth 2026

Also known as Freedom Day or Emancipation Day, Juneteenth is one of two freedom celebrations held throughout the United States; the other is the Fourth of July.

Making Space for Native Plants in Greater Eloise Stewardship Area

June 23 @ 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Afternoon Birding Stroll

Tuesdays, June 2-30, 4-5 pm Join a Naturalist for a late afternoon stroll on the trails in search of the various bird species that call the Garden home. Binoculars are available to borrow. Learn to identify birds by their plumage and songs while walking among the flowers. No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone […]

5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Juneteenth Celebration: Movie “Harriet”
June 23 @ 5:30 pm - 8:00 pm
Juneteenth Celebration: Movie “Harriet”

Come join Logan Park for an event honoring Juneteenth! We will be watching "Harriet", Harriet (2019) dramatizes Harriet Tubman’s daring escape from slavery and her transformation into a fearless conductor on the Underground Railroad. We will have a discussion afterwards. We will also have Juneteenth trivia to learn more about the history of Juneteenth Holiday!

Downtown Parks & Netflix Present: KPop Demon Hunters Sing-Along at The Commons

Grab your lightsticks and join Minneapolis Downtown Parks and Netflix for an exclusive outdoor screening of the official KPop Demon Hunters Sing-Along at The Commons Park in Downtown Minneapolis. 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Invertebrate Discovery Walks
June 24 @ 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm Recurring
Invertebrate Discovery Walks

Wednesdays, June 3 – June 24, 4:00pm – 5:00pm Join a naturalist on a walk to discover the hidden kingdoms of insects and other invertebrates in the Garden! We'll learn about invertebrate identification, their behavior, and ecology as we observe them on the trails. No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

Park Board Announcements

There are no announcements at this time. Please check back soon.

Explore Our Parks

Established in 1883, the Minneapolis Park and Recreation Board (MPRB) oversees a renowned urban park system spanning 7,059 acres of parkland and water. Featured among its 185 park properties are 55 miles of parkways, 102 miles of Grand Rounds biking and walking paths, 22 lakes, 12 formal gardens, seven golf courses and 49 recreation centers. Altogether, MPRB properties receive more than 30 million visits annually.

Learn more about us > Minneapolis is the Nation's Best Park System! > Departments & Leadership >
